title: Wedelia hirsuta
taxonomy: Wedelia hirsuta is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Wedelia, which is a group of plants commonly known as the Mexican daisy. The family Asteraceae is a large and diverse group of plants, often referred to as the sunflower or daisy family. The classification of Wedelia hirsuta reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other plants in the same family and genus. This classification system is based on the principles of taxonomy and is used to organize and understand the diversity of plant life.
uses: Wedelia hirsuta is used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, inflammation, and skin conditions. Its leaves and stems contain bioactive compounds that have been found to exhibit antibacterial and antifungal properties. The plant is also used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach ulcers. In addition, Wedelia hirsuta is believed to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help to reduce inflammation and improve overall health. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and potential uses of this plant.

side_effects: Wedelia hirsuta, also known as hairy wedelia or creeping zinnia, is a plant native to the Americas.
